stories.body: Thai officials say a fire that erupted near the stage of the Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao bar in Bangkok’s Chatuchak district late Sunday killed at least 27 people, left 22 others in critical condition and injured more than 60 as flammable ceiling decor, a blackout and suspected obstructions at an emergency exit trapped patrons, many of whom died from smoke inhalation while sheltering in bathrooms; authorities are investigating whether an air-conditioner short circuit sparked the blaze and have promised a full forensic review amid renewed scrutiny of lax fire-safety enforcement.
stories.headline: Bangkok bar inferno kills 27 as probe focuses on electrical faults and blocked exits

stories.body: After Iran claimed to have closed the Strait of Hormuz by firing on ships it deemed off-route, US Central Command said it launched more than 300 strikes over three nights and hit 140 Iranian military targets Saturday to protect commercial traffic, prompting Iran to retaliate with missiles and drones against US-linked facilities in Qatar, Kuwait, Jordan, Oman and Bahrain while energy markets reacted with Brent crude jumping above $78 per barrel and maritime trackers noting traffic through the vital waterway has plunged to single digits as Tehran insists it alone will guarantee safe passage once Washington withdraws.
stories.headline: US and Iran trade hundreds of strikes as Tehran vows to keep Strait of Hormuz shut

stories.body: On the day Iran buried slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Revolutionary Guards said they struck US Patriot batteries in Kuwait, an early-warning site in Qatar, a fuel depot in Bahrain and the Azraq base in Jordan in response to US raids that Tehran says killed 14 people; the clashes, which also reportedly hit the perimeter of Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant and disrupted shipping lanes the Guards insist must reopen under Iranian terms, came as President Donald Trump declared the interim ceasefire “over” even while arguing escalation will be brief and limited.
stories.headline: Iran touts attacks on US bases as Khamenei laid to rest amid ceasefire collapse

stories.body: Typhoon Bavi roared ashore in Zhejiang province near Yuhuan late Saturday before weakening inland, but China’s meteorological agency warned the France-sized system could dump days of torrential rain across multiple eastern and northern provinces after toppling more than 1,300 trees, flooding streets and disrupting thousands of flights and train services, while Taiwan reported 134 storm-related injuries and 137 international flight cancellations as authorities brace for further flooding in already saturated regions.
stories.headline: Typhoon Bavi forces 2.8 million evacuations in eastern China and batters Taiwan

stories.body: Three people familiar with the Kremlin told Reuters that President Vladimir Putin has rebuffed advisers who floated a ceasefire along current lines and is instead set on seizing the remainder of Ukraine’s Donbas region, potentially ordering broader operations — even strikes on NATO-linked sites — despite domestic fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian refineries, while Kyiv’s intelligence reportedly sees preparations for new offensives as the grinding front-line fight enters its fifth year.
stories.headline: Sources say Putin rejects peace overtures and prepares Donbas escalation

stories.body: French authorities deployed two Canadairs, helicopters and 400 firefighters to battle a fast-moving blaze in the Fontainebleau forest about 60 km southeast of Paris that has scorched roughly 800 hectares, forced evacuations in villages such as Vaudoue, partially shut the A6 motorway and caused high-speed rail delays of up to six hours as the Île-de-France endures its third heatwave and the interior minister warns nationwide burned acreage is already twice last year’s pace.
stories.headline: Wildfire south of Paris triggers aerial firefighting and rail chaos during heatwave

stories.body: NASA said it briefly directed five astronauts to shelter in their Crew Dragon spacecraft Friday when an air leak in Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to two pounds of air per day and Roscosmos cosmonauts prepared to cut into the area with a saw, a method US officials feared could worsen the breach; the safe-haven order was lifted after Roscosmos paused its fix and sealed one of two leaks, with both agencies now seeking a joint approach as the 27-year-old station again avoids an evacuation.
stories.headline: NASA rescinds ISS evacuation order after dispute over Russian leak repair plan

stories.body: Rep. Sylvia Garcia said acting ICE Director David Venturella confirmed that Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a 35-year Houston resident nearing legal status, was not the intended target of a Tuesday enforcement action in which an ICE officer fatally shot the unarmed homebuilder, prompting vigils, a makeshift memorial and renewed scrutiny of the raid as the agency investigates the shooting.
stories.headline: Family says Mexican builder killed by ICE agent in Houston was collateral victim

stories.body: Meteorologists say a vast, stubborn ridge of high pressure will park over the northern Plains this weekend and then wobble east, driving temperatures 15 to 25 degrees Fahrenheit above normal for up to two-thirds of the Lower 48, with triple-digit heat stretching from Nevada to the Dakotas, more than 90 daily records poised to fall — two-thirds of them overnight — and public health officials urging residents to hydrate, seek cooling centers and remember that oppressive nighttime warmth prevents bodies from recovering.
stories.headline: Forecasters warn of weeklong US heat dome bringing record daytime and nighttime highs

stories.body: After 6 to 12 inches of rain lashed southern Missouri on Friday, flash floods washed out roads, collapsed a Camp Taum Sauk building and trapped over 200 children and staff who were airlifted by Army National Guard Black Hawks, while officials confirmed a woman died in the deluge and warned that additional storms sweeping toward the Ohio and Tennessee valleys could trigger more flash flooding across the multistate region.
stories.headline: Missouri flooding kills one and forces helicopter rescue of more than 200 campers

stories.body: As the Popular Party alleges the updated Democratic Memory Law enabling descendants of Franco-era exiles to claim citizenship amounts to electoral engineering, the government reminded critics that the provision has been in force four years, endured 32 parliamentary steps, nine plenary debates and 469 amendments — 93 from the PP — and that the clarifying instruction was published in the official gazette in October 2022 after granting 544,722 passports under transparent procedures.
stories.headline: Government defends ‘grandchildren law’ after PP accuses it of rushed mass naturalizations

stories.body: Residents of Bédar and surrounding hamlets surveyed charred hillsides, swept ash from porches and mourned 13 neighbors whose bodies were found burned in Andalusia’s deadliest wildfire on record, describing anger at perceived inaction while authorities detailed how wind, dry brush and dispersed homes turned a single spark into a lethal inferno that firefighters and the military emergency unit battled across ravines and terraced fields.
stories.headline: Los Gallardos death toll rises to 13 as Almería residents return to devastated villages

stories.body: Five judges on Madrid’s Provincial Court began deliberating crucial appeals that could halt or endorse Judge Juan Carlos Peinado’s effort to put Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife on trial for alleged influence peddling, a case driven solely by far-right private accusers such as Hazte Oír and Vox after prosecutors declined, with the appellate panel previously reining in Peinado’s expansive investigation yet allowing it to proceed as he imposed measures like seizing Gómez’s passport.
stories.headline: Madrid appeals court weighs whether Begoña Gómez must face trial in corruption probe

stories.body: Catalonia’s latest Centre d’Estudis d’Opinió barometer shows separatist parties could regain 67 to 74 seats largely because xenophobic Aliança Catalana would leap from two deputies to as many as 25 by siphoning support from Junts, though forming an independence-front government remains unlikely given explicit vetoes from ERC and the CUP and lingering doubts inside Junts about working with Ripoll mayor Sílvia Orriols’s party ahead of elections still at least two years away.
stories.headline: Far-right Aliança Catalana surge puts pro-independence majority back within reach, CEO poll finds

stories.body: Officials said residents of eight buildings around Teodora Lamadrid, Rubinstein and Sant Gervasi de Cassoles streets must remain out at least until Saturday while engineers monitor three days of ground data, inspect cracks and inject cement around the eight-meter-wide, four-meter-deep void caused by L9 metro tunneling, with merchants demanding the project’s insurer meet them this week to guarantee compensation for lost business before any staggered return begins.
stories.headline: Barcelona sinkhole keeps 93 homes evacuated as L9 tunneling crews stabilize ground

stories.body: Meteocat data show that from January 1 to July 11 the Raval, Observatori Fabra, Zona Universitària and ZAL del Prat stations collectively registered 139 nights with minimum temperatures at or above 20°C, up from 126 over the same period last year, underscoring how humid nighttime heat now blankets the metropolitan area — notably with the traditionally cooler, higher-altitude Fabra already recording 33 tropical nights, the most in the series — and erasing the historic thermal contrast between mountain and sea.
stories.headline: Barcelona logs record number of tropical nights across four weather stations in 2026

stories.body: With the municipal shadow program delayed beyond July for the only playground atop Torre Baró hill, about 20 families and the residents’ association pooled roughly €120 to buy and mount shade cloths over the Castellví Street play area known as La Gàbia, saying local children had no alternative nearby and arguing the site still needs a full renovation with equipment for younger kids after years of the community providing shared toys and even kiddie pools to cope with extreme heat.
stories.headline: Torre Baró neighbors crowdfund and install shade sails at playground amid delays in city plan
